title: Finite Adams-Bashforth Method Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Adams-Bashforth bad multistep QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational two-step Adams-Bashforth replay for y' = 2t with h = 1/2 computes first multistep next_state = 1, while a malformed row claims next_state = 3/4, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional multistep time-stepping rows only when they add distinct history initialization, stability polynomial, variable-step, floating-point, or convergence-theorem pressure; keep general linear multistep theory in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed ODE, step size, exact starter value, history derivatives, Adams-Bashforth slopes, state updates, exact grid solution, and zero finite error table as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false first Adams-Bashforth multistep update through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact next state.
Expose the pack through finite dynamics/time-stepping, numerical-analysis, real-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general Adams-Bashforth order, consistency, zero-stability, convergence, variable-step behavior, floating-point time-stepping correctness, and PDE method-of-lines theory marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
